description:
In extract_client_ip (utils.cr), when TRUSTED_PROXY is enabled, the code validates X-Forwarded-For IPs but only trusts them if they come from internal ranges. However, if an attacker can somehow inject a fake X-Forwarded-For header with an internal IP, and TRUSTED_PROXY is set (even wrongly), they could bypass IP-based rate limiting.

**Location:** src/utils.cr:extract_client_ip

**Current behavior:**
1. Checks if TRUSTED_PROXY is set (via env var)
2. If set, checks X-Forwarded-For
3. Only trusts IPs from internal ranges or localhost

**Issue:** Even with the internal IP check, if an attacker can send a request with X-Forwarded-For: 127.0.0.1, and TRUSTED_PROXY is somehow enabled (misconfiguration), the rate limiting would be bypassed using loopback IP.

**Severity:** Medium - requires TRUSTED_PROXY to be set, which should only happen in controlled environments.

**Recommendation:** Add a warning log when X-Forwarded-For is used, similar to the existing one for TRUSTED_PROXY being enabled.
title: Bug: extract_client_ip TRUSTED_PROXY bypass for internal IPs
